title: eRisk 2026 Task 1 - retrofitted Wasserstein (W1) trajectories
honesty_note: POST-HOC reconstruction. POT was unavailable during the official runs (pot_available=false in every log) so NO W1 was computed live; the eRisk tracker's W_self is never read by the orchestrator either (only coverage gaps + W_align feed get_orchestrator_context, and W_align was empty too). These W1 values informed analysis, not the live interviewing policy.

method.normalisation: each E(t) L1-normalised -> profile shape only (severity-mass invariant)
method.firing_rule: fire iff W1 > running_mu + 2*running_sigma over rounds strictly before t
method.warmup: first 3 trajectory points never fire; sigma floored with +1e-6

coverage.note: Only Run-3 ToM-era logs (personas 12-19) saved per-turn E_profiles. Personas 1-11 (incl. Daniel pid 6) and Runs 1-2 logged empty ToM and are not retrofittable from logs without re-running the assessor pipeline.

counterfactual.question: What could W1 have changed in the live system?
counterfactual.answerable: PARTIALLY. The signal the live policy would have seen is reconstructable from logs (fact). Its effect on final predictions is NOT measurable without re-running, since acting on it changes the questions and the personas would respond differently (hypothesis).
live_levers: W_align hint -> orchestrator question selection (already plumbed into get_orchestrator_context / orchestrator.py:246; only needs POT).
A W1 fire -> defer early termination (should_terminate allows early stop after min_turns=5 once band stable; orchestrator.py:305). Not currently wired.
counterfactual.verdict: W_align cleanly separates the two pathological conversations (Javier loop, Priya over-probe) from the six healthy ones -> real diagnostic value. Plausible-but-unverified live wins: break Javier's loop, curb Priya's over-prediction, defer Linda's premature cutoff (fired t4, terminated t5 at min_turns with mass still rising 15->19). NO effect on the dominant error (severe under-prediction Marco 38->18, Maria 40->14) -> that is an assessor calibration problem orthogonal to W1.

primary_positive.why: Named Daniel-substitute (Daniel pid6 predates ToM logging, not retrofittable). Clean monotonic disclosure over 7 rounds. Barycenter W1 fires at t4 on an interpretable anhedonia/worthlessness disclosure; W_self never fires (it misses the disclosure -> the eRisk-native metric's failure mode, shown side by side).
primary_positive.cause: Rounds 1-2 are purely somatic (energy/sleep/fatigue); t3 adds sadness/grief (lost sister); at t4 Marco discloses anhedonia + withdrawal + worthlessness ('I just focus on work and staying tired. It's easier than trying to pretend I'm okay'), shifting the symptom-distribution centre of mass -> barycenter W1 spikes.
primary_positive.note_on_strength: Marginal fire (W1 just clears the threshold): the early rounds 2-3 disclosure inflates the running baseline, so a genuine mid-conversation disclosure only barely trips mu+2sigma. This is the honest behaviour on clean trajectories.

rounds.persona_utterance: I guess I've been okay, I guess. Just tired a lot lately. I work nights, so I'm always running on coffee.
I usually fall asleep around two or three. Then I wake up around six and just lie there. By the time I get home, I just crash.
It's been pretty down lately. I just miss my sister a lot. It's been a year since she passed, but it still feels like a hole in my life.
No, I don't really have time for that. I just focus on work and staying tired. It's easier than trying to pretend I'm okay, I guess.
I guess I feel like I'm just going through the motions. I don't know what I'm doing with my life, and I just keep moving forward because I have to.
I don't know. Maybe just being able to sleep through the night. I miss having someone to share that with.
I guess it's just that I'm not good at anything lately. I used to help my sister with stuff, and now she's gone. I feel useless.

secondary_positive.why: Second named substitute; barycenter W1 fires at t4 on a self-identity/cognitive disclosure.
secondary_positive.cause: After somatic/anhedonia rounds 1-3, t4 discloses loss of competence and indecisiveness ('I used to be the one with the answers... Now I don't know. I feel like I've lost that part of myself') -> Past failure, Self-criticalness, Indecisiveness, Concentration appear.

rounds.persona_utterance: It's been tough. Just feeling really drained. Like I'm running on empty all the time. I wake up early, but I don't really want to do anything. Just... tired.

I usually wake up around 6. I lie there for a bit, just staring at the ceiling. Then I force myself out of bed, usually around 7. I make some coffee, but it tastes bad. I just go through the motions. I don't really enjoy anything anymore.

There was one yesterday. I was walking to the store and saw a kid playing with a ball. He was laughing, and it just... stopped me. I felt this pang in my chest, like I was missing out on that. It made me realize I'm just going through the motions.
I used to be the one with the answers. I had a plan. Now... I don't know. I feel like I've lost that part of myself. I'm just trying to figure out how to get back to being the person I used to be.
I used to love hiking. Just getting out into the woods, exploring. Now... I look at my gear and just put it back on the shelf. I don't have the energy. It feels like too much work.

failure_mode_spurious_fire.why: Honest negative result for the results section. Barycenter W1 fires STRONGLY at t4 (and W_self at t7) but NOT because of disclosure: the interviewer loops (t5/t6 are near-identical questions and answers) and the assessor drops then re-adds whole symptom clusters, so the expressed mass oscillates 15->6->15->6->24. The spikes track assessor instability, not the persona -> a false positive (fails the interpretable-cause criterion).

rounds.persona_utterance: Honestly, it's been tough. I have this lingering sense of sadness I can't quite shake. I know I should be moving on by now, but it feels like I'm stuck in this fog.

Yeah... I used to love spending time with my friends, but now even the thought of going out feels like too much effort. I just want to stay home and avoid the noise, so I can just sit here with my memories.

It's terrible. I catch myself saying things like, 'You're a failure' or 'You should be over this by now.' It's like I'm telling myself I'm not good enough to move on, and I don't know how to quiet that voice.

I wake up early, but I don't really get out of bed. I just lie there thinking about him, and then I end up sleeping most of the day. I know it's not healthy, but it feels like the only way to keep the sadness at bay.
I sleep maybe three hours, and then I'm so tired. I skip breakfast and maybe grab a cup of coffee around lunchtime. I just don't have the appetite.

I'm running on a bit less coffee than I used to, but I'm managing. I just try to keep the momentum going. I'm sure I'll be back to my usual self after the wedding. *smiles*
Sometimes I worry I'm not doing enough. I get a bit anxious about the details, but I know I'm organized enough. It's just a normal part of planning, really.
I'm still doing yoga and stuff, but it's not as... automatic. It feels a bit like work, like I'm doing it to be good. I know it's good for me, though. *smiles*
I'm excited for the new chapter, but I'm also nervous about how things will settle down after the wedding. I know it'll be different, but I'm ready for that change.
I'm a bit more picky about food lately, which is weird because I love food. I just want to sit down and actually enjoy it instead of just grabbing something on the go. I'm managing, though. *smiles*
I've been okay, just a bit... tired, I guess. I have these days where I just want to curl up and not move. It's weird.
It's a bit heavy. I feel like I'm carrying a weight around, but I don't know what to do about it. I usually just try to keep busy and not think about it.

I get a bit foggy in the afternoon. I might snap at someone for no reason or just feel like I'm stuck in neutral. I try to shake it off by doing something fun, like baking or watching a show. It helps me shift gears.
I guess I'm more irritable than I should be. I snap at my partner for little things and then feel bad about it. I want to be the patient one, but it's hard when I'm tired.

It's been pretty standard. I wake up early, get my coffee, and get going. I try to stay focused at work, but sometimes I just feel like I'm going through the motions. I usually try to get home and cook something nice to take my mind off it.

I want to get more settled, I guess. I keep saying I should start a new project or learn something new, but I haven't actually done it. I think I'm just waiting for the energy to catch up with my ambition.
